grapes: Cabernet Sauvignon, Cabernet Franc, Merlot, Malbec, Petit Verdot
fallback_tasting_notes: Cross-vintage / house style: opaque ruby; dark-fruit aromatics (cassis, black cherry, blackberry, plum); fruit-forward, plush palate with moderate, well-integrated, gravelly tannins. Producer's house style is 'approachable young but age-worthy,' explicitly avoiding the massive tannin structure common to mountain Napa Cabs. No 2021-specific professional note located.

wine: Porter Family Vineyards Cave Dwellers Blend
tasting_notes: Producer (Porter Family Vineyards, 2019 tech sheet): "One of our favorite vintages ever produced... This year the volume turns up! Intense aromas of dark cherries, black berries and coffee grounds combine with a full texture and a broad mouthfeel. The Cabernet Sauvignon comes through a bit more in this release than others. Very approachable at a young age but sure to cellar nicely 12-15 years."

wine: Aonair Dad's Block Estate Cabernet Sauvignon Storyteller's Reserve
fallback_tasting_notes: 2022 vintage (producer copy): "Provocative aromas of wild blackberries, spiced mulberries, baker's chocolate, black truffle, crushed rock, lilacs, Turkish tobacco, and hints of creme brulee." House style: approachable young but age-worthy, avoids the massive tannins common to mountain Napa Cabs. Dad's Block is Aonair's flagship 'Storyteller's Reserve' tier (top of range, small allocation). No 2021-specific professional note located.

soil: ~Grey-brown sandy clay loam over red clay
fermentation_vessel: Old French oak barrels (whole-bunch pressed direct to barrel, no clarification)
tasting_notes: Philip Rich, Halliday Wine Companion (96 pts): "From vines planted in '82. Whole-bunched pressed to oak. The skins are frozen (to be used in the Dry Red Wine No. 2). A little more restrained than the '22. Aromas of perfectly ripened apricots and a touch of dried honey lead onto the palate, which is gently textured without being remotely oily. Long. Excellent."

fallback_tasting_notes: Cross-vintage / community composite: cherry and jammy fruit, well-balanced with strong tannins. Big, bold, rich; crisp full body with deep red fruit. Notes of dried cherry, blackstrap molasses, leather, slight earth/smoke. Nose: violet, lavender, graham cracker, crushed blackberries. House style: approachable young but age-worthy; avoids massive mountain-Cab tannins. No 2021-specific professional note located.

tasting_notes: Dave Thompson, Napa Wine Project: "The 2022 Aonair Cabernet Sauvignon Reserve, Napa Valley is deep ruby and opaque; immediately open knitted, ripe, but spicy, the nose offers aromas of blackberry jam, boysenberry spread, Himalayan purple mulberry, dark cherries, black licorice and dark plum... flavors of dark plum, blackberry, boysenberry, Persian mulberry, dried sage, dried bay leaf and dust, with gravelly and lightly grainy tannins and medium acidity. Mid-weight body, well-resolved texture — never heavy."

fallback_tasting_notes: House style across vintages: Aonair builds wines to be approachable young but age-worthy, explicitly avoiding the massive tannin structure common to mountain Napa Cabs. Across the Reserve Series Cabs, consistent house profile: deep ruby, dark-fruit-forward (blackberry, plum, boysenberry), gravelly tannins, mid-weight body, medium acidity, never heavy. 2023 Napa was cool and late — expect brighter acidity and more restrained ripeness than the 2021/2022 bottlings. No 2023-specific professional note located.

tasting_notes: Producer (Porter Family Vineyards, 2023 tech sheet): "Arguably our finest Syrah to date, the 2023 vintage is bursting with energy and spice. Aromas of lavender, cardamom and sage lead into a deeply layered palate of blackberry, mocha and allspice, with accents of white pepper, cinnamon and a touch of blood orange. Savory and beautifully balanced, this Syrah is both powerful and approachable, with a long, spicy finish. While delicious now, it will age gracefully over the next 10-15 years."

fallback_tasting_notes: Cross-vintage / Ceja Merlot house profile: raspberry, hint of root beer, clove, nuance of cardamom; lively, fresh red-fruited finish with a light herbal component. Pairs with carne de puerco con chile per winery. No 2021-specific professional note located.

Cross-vintage / Ceja Merlot house profile: raspberry, hint of root beer, clove, nuance of cardamom; lively, fresh red-fruited finish with a light herbal component. Pairs with carne de puerco con chile per winery. No 2021-specific professional note located.

Cross-vintage / Ceja Merlot house profile: raspberry, hint of root beer, clove, nuance of cardamom; lively, fresh red-fruited finish with a light herbal component. Pairs with carne de puerco con chile per winery. No 2021-specific professional note located.

House style across vintages: Aonair builds wines to be approachable young but age-worthy, explicitly avoiding the massive tannin structure common to mountain Napa Cabs. Across the Reserve Series Cabs, consistent house profile: deep ruby, dark-fruit-forward (blackberry, plum, boysenberry), gravelly tannins, mid-weight body, medium acidity, never heavy. 2023 Napa was cool and late — expect brighter acidity and more restrained ripeness than the 2021/2022 bottlings. No 2023-specific professional note located.

wine: Ten Minutes by Tractor Coolart Road Pinot Noir
fallback_tasting_notes: Producer copy for the Coolart Road bottling (used across vintages): "Spice and raspberry coulis plentiful on the nose before a more floral, blackcurrant and umami palate; flavours work with the small portion of whole-bunch fermented wine creating a sultry feel with a chewy savoury tannin edge." Coolart Road is one of Ten Minutes by Tractor's six estate vineyards on the Mornington Peninsula. No 2024-specific professional note located.

House style across vintages: Aonair builds wines to be approachable young but age-worthy, explicitly avoiding the massive tannin structure common to mountain Napa Cabs. Across the Reserve Series Cabs, consistent house profile: deep ruby, dark-fruit-forward (blackberry, plum, boysenberry), gravelly tannins, mid-weight body, medium acidity, never heavy. 2023 Napa was cool and late — expect brighter acidity and more restrained ripeness than the 2021/2022 bottlings. No 2023-specific professional note located.

grapes: Cabernet Sauvignon, Cabernet Franc, Merlot, Malbec, Petit Verdot
fallback_tasting_notes: House style across vintages: Aonair's Mountains Proprietary is a Bordeaux-style blend from Napa's mountain AVAs — opaque ruby, dark-fruit aromatics (cassis, black cherry, blackberry, plum), plush, fruit-forward palate with moderate, well-integrated, gravelly tannins. Producer's style is 'approachable young but age-worthy,' explicitly avoiding the massive tannin structure common to mountain Napa Cabs. 2023 Napa was cool and late (widely called 'Vintage of a Lifetime') — expect brighter acidity, longer hang-time complexity, and more restrained ripeness than the 2021. No 2023-specific professional note located.

wine: Porter Family Vineyards Atlas Peak Cabernet Sauvignon (Circle R Ranch)
soil: ~Volcanic rhyolitic tuff, red volcanic rock, loam
tasting_notes: Producer (Porter Family Vineyards, 2023 tech sheet): "Expressive aromas of blackcurrant and blackberry lead, accented by hints of tar, cedar, and aromatic wood. The palate unfolds with layers of chocolate-covered cherry and savory spice, supported by firm, structured tannins. Long and persistent on the finish, this Cabernet reflects the power and depth of Atlas Peak with balance and age-worthy character."

grapes: 42% Cabernet Sauvignon, 31% Merlot, 14% Cabernet Franc, 8% Malbec, 5% Petit Verdot
tasting_notes: Producer (Porter Family Vineyards, 2023 tech sheet): "A richly layered blend with aromas of huckleberry and pomegranate, accented by baking spice and clove. The palate is juicy and mouthcoating, with a broad, round mid-palate that unfolds into cocoa and savory depth. Textural and chewy, this wine carries nice length and a long finish. Enjoy now for its generosity, or cellar confidently for the next 15 years."

wine: Yarra Yering Dry Red Wine No. 1
grapes: 58% Cabernet Sauvignon, 22% Merlot, 15% Malbec, 5% Petit Verdot
soil: ~Grey-brown sandy clay loam over red clay
fermentation_vessel: Half-tonne fermenters; 1/3 Cab in 3500L new French upright cask, 8 weeks on skins
tasting_notes: Producer (Yarra Yering, 2023 Dry Red No. 1): "A melange of summer berry fruits and a comforting rustic iodine feel complement some bay leaf lift. Warming clove spice, a hint of graphite and some oak char bring both flavour and tannin. Beautiful fruit weight, refreshing acidity and fine tannins form a solid foundation for a long future. Complex and layered, but not intimidating, always exciting to watch evolve." | Philip Rich, Halliday Wine Companion (99 pts, Cabernet & Blends of the Year 2026): "Deep, bright purple with classic cool-climate cabernet aromas of black cherry, blackcurrants, cedar, licorice and subtle iodine. The palate is simultaneously concentrated, structured and refined, finishing with an ironstone minerality."

wine: Porter Family Vineyards Estate Cabernet Sauvignon
tasting_notes: Producer (Porter Family Vineyards, 2016 tech sheet): "Coombsville fruit at its best. Subtle notes of black cherry, cocoa, cedar and tea leaves evolve into an opulent mid-palate with soft and well-integrated tannins. Its lovely texture combined with a long finish create a wine that is extremely approachable when young, with the potential to develop nicely in years to come. Drink at release or enjoy its evolution over the next 10-15 years."

wine: Porter Family Vineyards Estate Cabernet Sauvignon
tasting_notes: Producer (Porter Family Vineyards, 2016 tech sheet): "Coombsville fruit at its best. Subtle notes of black cherry, cocoa, cedar and tea leaves evolve into an opulent mid-palate with soft and well-integrated tannins. Its lovely texture combined with a long finish create a wine that is extremely approachable when young, with the potential to develop nicely in years to come. wine: Porter Family Vineyards Estate Cabernet Sauvignon
tasting_notes: Producer (Porter Family Vineyards, 2022 tech sheet): "The 2022 Cabernet offers a vibrant profile of black currants, cherry and wild berries, layered with cedar, anise, dark chocolate and hints of Earl Grey tea. The texture is lush and polished—especially through the mid-palate. Bright and lifted, the finish is long and still evolving. This wine is ready to drink now and will cellar beautifully over the next 7-10 years."

grapes: 35% Cabernet Sauvignon, 35% Merlot, 20% Malbec, 9% Cabernet Franc, 1% Petit Verdot
tasting_notes: Producer (Porter Family Vineyards, 2017 tech sheet): "Deep and luscious, this Bordeaux-style blend showcases exotic notes of sandalwood, Indian spices, green tea and bittersweet chocolate. The palate offers a forward, fleshy texture and an elegant long finish. Enjoy now or cellar for the next 15-20 years."

grapes: 35% Cabernet Sauvignon, 35% Merlot, 20% Malbec, 9% Cabernet Franc, 1% Petit Verdot
tasting_notes: Producer (Porter Family Vineyards, 2017 tech sheet): "Deep and luscious, this Bordeaux-style blend showcases exotic notes of sandalwood, Indian spices, green tea and bittersweet chocolate. The palate offers a forward, fleshy texture and an elegant long finish. Enjoy now or cellar for the next 15-20 years."

grapes: 35% Cabernet Sauvignon, 35% Merlot, 20% Malbec, 9% Cabernet Franc, 1% Petit Verdot
tasting_notes: Producer (Porter Family Vineyards, 2017 tech sheet): "Deep and luscious, this Bordeaux-style blend showcases exotic notes of sandalwood, Indian spices, green tea and bittersweet chocolate. The palate offers a forward, fleshy texture and an elegant long finish. Enjoy now or cellar for the next 15-20 years."

wine: Yarra Yering Dry Red Wine No. 1
grapes: 70% Cabernet Sauvignon, 18% Merlot, 8% Malbec, 4% Petit Verdot
soil: ~Grey-brown sandy clay loam over red clay
tasting_notes: James Halliday, Wine Companion (98 pts): "A dense and dark ruby decorates the glass, with vibrancy in the rim. Aromas led by dark cherry, blackberry, and plum notes, embraced by lilac, cassis, and camphor. The aromas and flavours are still fresh and vibrant, but the palate, in particular, brooks no argument: this is a wine for protracted cellaring to allow the whipcord-tight structure to relax its grip. Elegant mouthfeel, complex red fruit, joyful acidity, and well-knit tannic structure resolving in a long-lasting finish." | William Kelley, Wine Advocate (97+ pts): "One of the great Cabernet blends in Australia — collects it each vintage." | James Suckling: 97 pts.

wine: Ten Minutes by Tractor Trahere Pinot Noir
fermentation_vessel: Small stainless cubes (17-19 days on skins, destemmed)
tasting_notes: Producer (Ten Minutes by Tractor): "The 2022 Trahere Pinot Noir is the first wine crafted solely from our high-density Spedding Vineyard, a blend of three exceptional clones—Abel, Pommard, and Calera—grown across the 1.48 ha site. These clones thrived under the ideal 2022 harvest conditions, resulting in a wine of remarkable complexity and sophistication. Layers of vibrant red cherry, bramble spice, and blackberry surround silky tannins, driving a fruit intensity and structure that lingers beautifully on the palate." Ranked #1 Australian Pinot Noir of the 2022 vintage.

wine: Ten Minutes by Tractor 10X Pinot Gris
barrel_time: Concrete + older French oak barriques (post-ferment maturation)
tasting_notes: Producer (Ten Minutes by Tractor, 2025 10X Pinot Gris): "An alluring and perfumed gris with plenty of orchard fruits, musk and a touch of spice. Notes of rockmelon, tart crust and fennel provide an entrée to this aromatic wine, while the palate has a lovely soft texture with pops of white peach and cardamom with bright acidity providing length and drive." Blended from three estate-controlled vineyards: Hunts Road, Osborn, Wallis.

wine: S.R. Tonella Cellars Coombsville Cabernet Franc
fallback_tasting_notes: Producer background / house style: S.R. Tonella Cellars is a 4th-generation Rutherford producer making small-lot wines from benchland Napa vineyards. Their Cab Franc program (2019, 2022 vintages documented publicly) sources from Bennett Vineyard in Coombsville — cool marine-influenced AVA, structured-but-elegant Cab Franc profile. Coombsville appellation confirmed from the 2021 bottle label. No 2021-specific professional note located; this vintage does not appear in the producer's public release history, suggesting an allocation / library / one-off bottling.

Producer / winery context: Greater Fool Wine is a small Napa producer (Julian Christian & Kathryn Siegfried) known for co-fermentation and small-lot hand-crafted wines. This is the inaugural release of their varietal Cabernet Sauvignon — 58 cases, 100% Cab, Napa Valley AVA. Hand-picked Sept 11, 2022 (early, pre-over-ripening in a warm vintage); 18 months in 67% new French + Hungarian oak. No professional review located for this bottling. House style across their other reds trends toward fresh, juicy, red-fruited with rounded tannins. Back-label info captured from bottle.

wine: Painted Fields Curse of Knowledge Cabernet Sauvignon
tasting_notes: Producer (Painted Fields Wines, 2022 Curse of Knowledge tech sheet): "Curse of Knowledge strives to change perceptions of Sierra Foothills wine. Our high-quality, well-balanced Cabernet Sauvignon is created by blending Cabernet Franc and Merlot. It shows an intense ruby color that will captivate your senses, along with a sensational bouquet of dried violet, black cherry, fresh herbs, and cocoa powder. The velvety tannins create a generous and round, balanced mid-palate, followed by a lingering finish of dark berries and black licorice. Enjoy now to experience the brightness of the wine, or age it in your cellar to enhance its flavors." Tasting Panel magazine: 94 pts. Vineyards: Andis Estate + Rancho Victoria (Cab), Twin Oaks (Merlot), Hawk Creek (Cab Franc). Philippe Melka / Maayan Koschitzky consulting winemakers.

fallback_tasting_notes: Historical / vintage context: 1974 is one of California's most celebrated Cabernet vintages (alongside 1968, 1970, 1978) — top-tier 1974 Cabs from Heitz, Mayacamas, and Montelena have famously aged 50+ years and still drink well. Sebastiani's Proprietor's Reserve was their flagship tier in the 1970s, made during August Sebastiani's era — well-regarded but a step below the Heitz/Mayacamas/Montelena elite. Label reads 'Northern California' (broad-sourced, pre-AVA era generic designation) and 12.5% ABV — moderate alcohol typical of the 1970s and actually favorable for aging. At 52 years old, condition is everything: check fill level (high-shoulder or better), cork integrity, and capsule for seepage before opening. No vintage-specific professional note located.

wine: Sonoma Vineyards River West Old Vines Zinfandel (Estate Bottled)
fallback_tasting_notes: Historical / producer context: Rod Strong founded Sonoma Vineyards in 1959; renamed to Rodney Strong Vineyards in 1980 — so this 1977 bottling is from the original 'Sonoma Vineyards' era. River West Vineyard (Middle Reach of Russian River Valley) was first planted in the 1930s and contains 100-year-old Zinfandel vines; at the time of this bottling Strong was farming it under a long-term arrangement (official acquisition: 2023). Label legitimately uses 'Estate Bottled' designation because the winery controlled the vineyard operation even pre-ownership. Old-vine Russian River Zinfandel can age surprisingly well when stored properly; 1970s Zins from Ridge, Ravenswood, and similar old-vine sources have shown at 40+ years. 1977 was a solid (not legendary) California vintage. Subregion 'Russian River Valley' used here for geographic accuracy — the RRV AVA itself wasn't established until 1983, so the 1977 label likely just says 'Sonoma County'. No vintage-specific professional note located. At 49 years old, condition is everything — check fill, cork, and capsule before opening.

wine: Patland Vineyards Extended Barrel Aged Proprietary Red Wine
grapes: 61% Cabernet Sauvignon, 14% Malbec, 13% Syrah, 8% Merlot, 4% Petit Verdot
soil: Various (Stagecoach Atlas Peak + Hyde Carneros + Beckstoffer)
tasting_notes: Wine Access (retailer, 2022 release): "A dark ruby core in the glass. The aromas are led by rich dark fruit, including Damson plum and ripe black cherry, accented by licorice, tobacco, purple flowers, and a touch of cassis. The palate reflects the mountain fruit, with big dark fruit, depth, and spice carried on rich tannins that deliver a long-lasting finish." Vineyard sources: Stagecoach Vineyard (Atlas Peak), Hyde Vineyard (Carneros), Andy Beckstoffer site.

fallback_tasting_notes: Cross-vintage / Ceja Merlot house profile: raspberry, hint of root beer, clove, nuance of cardamom; lively, fresh red-fruited finish with a light herbal component. Pairs with carne de puerco con chile per winery. No 2021-specific professional note located.

Cross-vintage / Ceja Merlot house profile: raspberry, hint of root beer, clove, nuance of cardamom; lively, fresh red-fruited finish with a light herbal component. Pairs with carne de puerco con chile per winery. No 2021-specific professional note located.

Producer background / composition context: Maxenti Cellars appears related to S.R. Tonella Cellars (shared logo/branding; Steve Tonella involved). The 2023 Cabernet Sauvignon is 96% Cab Sauv / 4% Aglianico — an unusual small addition of the southern Italian variety (used in Taurasi, Aglianico del Vulture). Cross-county blend: 52% Napa Valley + 48% Sonoma County. No vintage-specific professional note located for this varietal bottling. Aglianico adds tannic structure and a savory/earthy note to the primary Cabernet character.

grapes: 60% Merlot, 25% Cabernet Sauvignon, 5% Malbec, 5% Petit Verdot, 5% Cabernet Franc
soil: ~Volcanic ash, decomposed volcanic, red clay loam
tasting_notes: Owen Bargreen (97 pts, drinking window 2024-2044): "This right bank inspired blend of Merlot and Cabernet Franc shows gorgeous red florals on the nose alongside wet stone, blackberry compote and creme de cassis with sweet pipe tobacco. The palate is soft and refined with serious texture and underlying verve." Cross-source vintage-specific detail: deep ruby; aromas of chocolate brownie, Santa Rosa plum, dark cherry, blackberry; flavors of red cherry, red plum, black berry, dark raspberry; grainy/granular tannins, vibrant finish. Backbone is Rockwell Ridge estate vineyard (1,365 ft, 35 ft below Howell Mountain AVA). Described as one of the finest releases of this bottling to date.

wine: S.R. Tonella Cellars Rutherford Cabernet Sauvignon
fallback_tasting_notes: Pre-release allocation via S.R. Tonella's Soda Canyon cave membership. Public release scheduled for 2026-10-06. No 2023-specific tasting note or tech specs available. Adjacent-vintage pattern (2021/2022 Rutherford Cab Sauv): 100% Cabernet Sauvignon from Rutherford Bench, 18 months in 100% French oak (70% new), $130 member price. 2022 Rutherford Cab received 94 pts from The Wine Palate. Producer context: Steve Tonella is 4th-generation Rutherford vintner; family has cultivated grapes in Rutherford since 1907. 2023 Napa vintage widely called 'Vintage of a Lifetime' — cool, long hang-time, classic structure, age-worthy.

wine: Yarra Yering Dry Red Wine No. 1
grapes: 70% Cabernet Sauvignon, 18% Merlot, 8% Malbec, 4% Petit Verdot
soil: ~Grey-brown sandy clay loam over red clay

wine: Ten Minutes by Tractor 10X Pinot Gris
barrel_time: Concrete + older French oak barriques (post-ferment maturation)
tasting_notes: Producer (Ten Minutes by Tractor, 2025 10X Pinot Gris): "An alluring and perfumed gris with plenty of orchard fruits, musk and a touch of spice. Notes of rockmelon, tart crust and fennel provide an entrée to this aromatic wine, while the palate has a lovely soft texture with pops of white peach and cardamom with bright acidity providing length and drive." wine: Ten Minutes by Tractor Trahere Pinot Noir
fermentation_vessel: Small stainless cubes (17-19 days on skins, destemmed)
tasting_notes: Producer (Ten Minutes by Tractor): "The 2022 Trahere Pinot Noir is the first wine crafted solely from our high-density Spedding Vineyard, a blend of three exceptional clones—Abel, Pommard, and Calera—grown across the 1.48 ha site. These clones thrived under the ideal 2022 harvest conditions, resulting in a wine of remarkable complexity and sophistication. Layers of vibrant red cherry, bramble spice, and blackberry surround silky tannins, driving a fruit intensity and structure that lingers beautifully on the palate." Ranked #1 Australian Pinot Noir of the 2022 vintage.

soil: ~Volcanic rhyolitic tuff, red volcanic rock, loam
tasting_notes: Sommeliers Choice Awards 2023 (Gold Medal, 93 pts): "Blueberries and black tar notes add dimension to the flavour of this red wine. Vanilla from the oak and soft tannins combine to offer a longer lasting finish. Full bodied Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon." Note: Forthright Winery's only estate vineyard is 1 acre on Mt. Veeder; Atlas Peak fruit for this bottling is sourced. Forthright produces at the Caves of Soda Canyon on Atlas Peak.

Producer (2025 tech sheet): "The 2025 Rosé opens with an enchanting bouquet of ripe peaches, raspberries, and delicate rose petals. On the palate, it is bright and energetic, with refreshing hints of blood orange and grapefruit. A subtle mineral undertone weaves through a clean, lingering finish. Perfect for savoring in the golden days of summer!" Whole-cluster pressed, fermented in 2-ton stainless steel with daily lees stirring for ~2 weeks, finished in neutral barrels.

Producer (2023 tech sheet, explicit cellaring guidance — 15 years from release): "A vibrant red blend with inviting aromas of tangerine peel, tea leaf, wild cherry, and fresh berries. The palate is round and textural, unfolding with layers of plum sauce and cocoa, framed by gentle dusty tannins. Long, smooth, and easy to drink, this wine offers immediate charm, yet can also be laid down for those with the patience to cellar it over the next 15 years." Producer calls 2023 the "Vintage of a Lifetime" — cool, late, long hang time, described as exceptional and age-worthy.

wine: Ten Minutes by Tractor McCutcheon Chardonnay
soil: Red ferrosols, silty clay loam topsoil
fermentation_vessel: French oak barriques + concrete, indigenous yeast
tasting_notes: Producer (2024 tech sheet, explicit drinking window 2025-2035): "Vibrant white stone fruit of peach and nectarine and a hint of struck match. A powerful yet refined palate highlights the depth of fruit and overall purity that is true to this site. The savoury and chalky edges are complex and the bright cut lemon linger is captivating." Clones: 52% Clone 95, 33% P58, 15% Clone 96. 10 months in French oak barriques (30% new) plus concrete. Hand-picked March 25-28, 2024; bottled February 2025 under screwcap. ABV 13.0%, pH 3.21, TA 6.4 g/L, yield 2 t/ha.

barrel_time: 5 months on lees in stainless steel
fallback_tasting_notes: Retailer listing (Primal Wine, 2024 product page — reads as house-style description): "Pale straw yellow with greenish highlights and good clarity. The nose is delicate and clean, with acacia blossom, wisteria, pear, and a subtle citrus note. The palate is fresh and taut, with mineral tension, a saline edge from the basalt soils, and a characteristically bitter finish." Primal guidance: drink within 3-4 years of vintage while freshness is at its peak. House-style language from Kermit Lynch sibling vintages: "lemongrass with a pinch of sea salt ... lemon zest, star fruit, wet stone, white flowers ... bone-dry." Vineyards: Brovia and Monti di Mezzo, ~25-year-old vines, organic/biodynamic, whole-cluster press. No 2024-specific professional note located.

wine: Porter Family Vineyards Beverly's Chardonnay (Toyon Farm)
tasting_notes: Producer (2023 tech sheet): "Aromas of green apple and lemon zest are complemented by subtle notes of peach and creamy vanilla bean. On the palate, a delightful honeydew melon character unfolds, supported by a round, textural body and vibrant acidity. A hint of minerality adds a refreshing, mouthwatering quality, while the finish is clean and exceptionally long. While it's delightful now, it will continue to evolve beautifully in the next 2-5 years." Whole-cluster pressed, ~30-day ferment in predominantly neutral oak, partial malolactic, batonnage. TA 6.8 g/L, RS 0.02%. Fruit sourced from Toyon Farm (34-acre Carneros grower, Napa side, neighbors Hyde/Cakebread/Bayview). Porter calls 2023 the "Vintage of a Lifetime" in Napa.

wine: Porter Family Vineyards Beverly's Chardonnay (Toyon Farm)
tasting_notes: Producer (2024 tech sheet): "Baked apples lead the nose, layered with pear, honeydew, grape blossom, and hints of lychee and lemon meringue. Subtle notes of dough and savory depth add complexity. The palate is beautifully balanced and textural, finishing long with mouthwatering minerality in a restrained, Old World Burgundian style." Whole-cluster pressed, ~30-day ferment in predominantly neutral oak, partial malolactic, batonnage. Fruit sourced from Toyon Farm (Carneros, Napa side). Final chemistry not published on 2024 tech sheet (2023 had full chem, Porter dropped it for 2024). 2024 growing season: heavy winter rains followed by 40+ days of intense summer heat; strong canopies preserved fruit quality, harvest back to typical timing after variable years.

grapes: Cabernet Sauvignon, Cabernet Franc, Merlot, Malbec, Petit Verdot
fallback_tasting_notes: House style across vintages: Aonair's Mountains Proprietary is a Bordeaux-style blend from Napa's mountain AVAs — opaque ruby, dark-fruit aromatics (cassis, black cherry, blackberry, plum), plush, fruit-forward palate with moderate, well-integrated, gravelly tannins. Producer's style is 'approachable young but age-worthy,' explicitly avoiding the massive tannin structure common to mountain Napa Cabs. 2023 Napa was cool and late (widely called 'Vintage of a Lifetime') — expect brighter acidity, longer hang-time complexity, and more restrained ripeness than the 2021. No 2023-specific professional note located.

tasting_notes: Dave (The Napa Wine Project, 2023): "Highly aromatic — the aromatics fly out of the glass immediately upon pouring. Its scents are a union of both red and darker fruits including Santa Rosa plum, dark raspberries, ripe blackberry and boysenberry. Fully ripe but with flavor, acidity and texture in harmony, this wine tastes like Satsuma plum, boysenberry, blackberry and Persian mulberry. The savory and dark finish lingers with grainy and gravelly textured tannins ultimately pacing the fruit on the finish.". | Producer/house context: Grant Long Jr. sources from iron-rich rocky soils of the El Dorado Sierra foothills; oak used as 'an integrating tool rather than a dominating one,' crafted smooth and drinkable young yet structured for aging. The 2017 showed well-evolved tertiary aromas (dark pepper, dried herbs, sage, tobacco, old cedar, leather) with resolved tannins, suggesting a ~7-yr-plus aging arc. Specific 2023 blend components and tech specs (ABV/pH/barrel program/cases) not publicly located.

grapes: Cabernet Sauvignon 78%, Merlot 8%, Petit Verdot 6%, Cabernet Franc 6%, Malbec 2%
tasting_notes: Galloni (Vinous, 97 pts): "The 2019 Opus One is a dense, beautifully layered wine, but it is also quite restrained. Tasted next to the 2018, the 2019 possesses a bit more textural nuance and body, yet it has all the classicism that is such an Opus One signature. Bright floral accents linger on the sublime finish." Drink 2029-2049. | Suckling (99 pts): "The purity of fruit is really something else here, with currants and fresh flowers, such as violets and roses." | Producer/house context: 19 months in 100% new French oak; avg production >25,000 cases/yr, 2019-specific case count not published.
